stage: A (sense-split break/make/find/friend; design §6.1 — stages B-D gated, not built)
authorship: research-grade, agent-authored against profile-1 per docs/next/design/sense-split-first-construction.md; NOT federation-endorsed; adequacy unvalidated pending V-A/V-B
encoderNote: READ-ONLY pin check: equals kernel-v0 frozen value; kernel-v1 is a data corpus — no encoder change, no schema change, no ALGORITHM_VERSION bump, no X0 golden regeneration (design §2.1)

concepts.label: break.interrupt (X breaks Y — Y is a continuing happening and because of what X does it stops; as in "break a streak" / "break the cycle", NOT as in "break a glass")
break.malfunction (X breaks Y — Y stops being able to do what it is for, though it stays in one piece; as in "break the alarm clock", NOT as in "break a glass" or "break a promise")
break.shatter-become (Y breaks — Y comes apart into many small pieces, with no breaker said; as in "the figurine broke", NOT as in "he broke the glass")
break.shatter (X breaks Y — X does something to Y and Y comes apart into many small pieces; as in "break a glass", NOT as in "break a promise" or "break the silence")
break.violate (X breaks Y — Y is words such as a promise, a law, or a rule, and X does not do what these words say; as in "break a promise" / "break the law", NOT as in "break a glass")
find.discover (X finds Y — X comes to know something after not knowing it; as in "find a flaw" / "she found that he had lied", NOT as in "find the keys")
find.locate (X finds Y — X comes to know where Y is after not knowing; as in "find the keys", NOT as in "find a flaw" or "find that ...")
friend.figurative (X is a friend of Y — X is not a person, but Y thinks about X the way one thinks about a friend; as in "imaginary friend" / a beloved toy or dog spoken of as a friend)
friend.person (X is a friend of Y — X and Y are people who know each other well with affection and trust; as in "my best friend", NOT as in "imaginary friend" or a toy spoken of as a friend)
make.cause (X makes Y do something — because of what X does, Y does it; as in "the ads made me buy it" / "she made him apologize", NOT as in "make a chair")
make.create (X makes Y — because of what X does, Y comes to exist; as in "make a chair" / "make a cake", NOT as in "make someone sad" or "make the bed" or "make the train")

gates.G1_note: must be 0; a merge is reported as a measured profile-1 expressivity limit, never silently absorbed
gates.pi011_class_unmintable_check: PASS (no break.violate range record anchored at material entity)

urn:kernel-v0:break.note: agent sort deliberately broadened SomeoneRef->SomethingRef per design §3.2; sibling senses authored fresh

urn:kernel-v0:friend.note: figurative sibling minted per adjudication §B.iii 040/041 flag

deprecationNote: kernel-v0 is frozen forever and is NOT modified; these four v0 concepts are superseded for NEW consumers only. Stage C owns the total deprecation map + repo grep gate (design §6.2 risk 6).

typing.maintainerClause: every break.* sense is ontic_category=event (checked fail-closed at build)

minting.mintToolHashNote: sha256 over tools/mint/src/*.ts AT BUILD TIME. kernel-v0 was minted at e07bc2ac…; the tool sources have since gained corpus specs (physics/math/etc). The ALGORITHM is proven unchanged by the continuityChecks block: re-minting the four frozen v0 concepts from committed bytes reproduces their frozen URNs exactly.
minting.identityRootAlgorithm: binary Merkle over unique minted URNs sorted by UTF-16 code unit; leaf=sha256(utf8(urn)); parent=sha256(left||right); odd node promoted; hex root
minting.referenceMode: substitute (no intra-corpus references in Stage A; all singletons)
minting.identityPayload: Identity = the kot-ast/1 `explication` object (content-addressed by meaning). label/gloss/notes/pattern/status/references AND the `sense` block are annotation (design §2.1: senseTag/alias outside identity).
